blurb: The five primary pricing architectures for SaaS businesses: Freemium, Flat-Rate, Tiered, Per-User, and Usage-Based.

title: SaaS Rule of 40
blurb: The SaaS Rule of 40 is a business heuristic that asserts a healthy SaaS company's growth rate plus profit margin should sum to at least 40%.

title: Sales Enablement
blurb: Sales Enablement is the discipline of equipping sales teams with the knowledge, content, tools, training, and coaching they need to engage buyers effectively and close deals.

title: Sales-Led GTM
blurb: A Sales-Led Go-to-Market strategy makes the sales team the primary engine of customer acquisition.

title: Sales-Led vs Product-Led GTM
blurb: A Go-to-Market (GTM) methodology is the core mechanism by which a company acquires and converts customers.

title: Scalable Business Model
blurb: A scalable business model is one that can increase output — users, revenue, market reach — without a proportional increase in resources, costs, or operational complexity.

title: Servitization
blurb: Servitization is the strategic shift from selling products outright to selling services or outcomes around those products.

title: Single-Feature MVP
blurb: A Single-Feature MVP is a high-fidelity MVP that contains only the core feature of your future product — nothing else.

title: Six Business Model Shifts
blurb: A taxonomy of six structural shifts modern business models are undergoing — patterns of evolution that successful firms are riding while laggards risk obsolescence.

title: Six Types of Customer Relationships
blurb: A framework cataloguing the six structural modes a company can use to relate to its customers, drawn from the Business Model Canvas tradition and elaborated by Pereira.

title: Strategic Partnerships
blurb: Strategic partnerships are formal alliances between two or more organizations to achieve mutual business objectives that would be difficult or impossible to achieve independently.

title: Subscription Box Model
blurb: The Subscription Box model delivers a curated or replenishment shipment of physical goods to subscribers on a recurring schedule (typically monthly).

title: Subscription Business Model
blurb: A subscription business model is a revenue model in which customers pay a recurring fee — weekly, monthly, or yearly — to access a product or service.

title: Subscription Fatigue
blurb: Subscription fatigue is the state of consumer exhaustion caused by an oversupply of subscription offerings across products and services.

title: Subscription Maturity Model
blurb: The Subscription Maturity Model is a five-stage framework describing the operational evolution of a subscription-based business.

title: Subscription Revenue Cycle
blurb: The Subscription Revenue Cycle is a five-step operating framework describing how subscription businesses generate and compound revenue over time.

title: Switching Costs
blurb: Switching costs are the total cost — time, money, effort, risk — a customer incurs when moving from one provider's product to another's.

title: Take-Rate Economics
blurb: Take-rate economics describes how platforms, marketplaces, and brokerages capture value: by taking a percentage of each transaction they facilitate.

title: TAM SAM SOM Sizing
blurb: TAM/SAM/SOM is a three-layer market sizing tactic used to demonstrate that a market is large enough to support the financial investment required to pursue it.

title: White-Labeling Model
blurb: White-labeling is a revenue model where a producer manufactures or develops a product/service that another company then rebrands and sells as its own.
